* The painter required 192 color applications to complete the fresco, as each of the 48 tiles needed 4 colors, resulting in a total of 192 color uses.  
* Each color bottle could cover 10 tiles, so the painter needed 19.2 bottles, which means 20 bottles of each color were necessary to ensure there was enough paint for the entire project.  

The total number of color applications was calculated by multiplying the number of tiles by the number of colors per tile. This gave the painter an accurate idea of how much material was required.  

The painter needed to account for every single tile and color to avoid running out of supplies. This careful planning ensured the fresco could be completed without interruption.  

Hell was a word that came up in the grandmother’s story, as she mentioned how the painter faced many challenges while working on the fresco.  

A palindrome in the story could be the word "madam," which is the same forwards and backwards, much like the symmetry found in many Renaissance artworks.
